artifacts.description: Conservative extracted evidence table with regional adolescent tobacco-prevalence figures and survey-system metadata from WHO and CDC sources.
Short research log describing scope and limitations of the collected public evidence.

claims.notes: This is a regional estimate and should not be generalized to all of Asia without additional evidence.
claims.text: Authoritative WHO reporting located here is consistent with the WHO South-East Asia Region having a large share of the world's adolescent tobacco users; the cited figure is about 11 million users aged 13-15 years, or about 30% of the global total, using surveys conducted during 2012-2022 and applied to 2021 populations.

claims.notes: Figure text indicates the estimate is based on surveys from 2012-2022 and population application to 2021.
claims.text: The same WHO evidence is consistent with an average current tobacco-use prevalence around 11% among adolescents aged 13-15 years in the WHO South-East Asia Region.

claims.notes: These are older regional estimates and may not reflect current prevalence.
claims.text: Older CDC/WHO GYTS reporting suggests that, in South-East Asia, any tobacco use among students aged 13-15 years was about 12.9% and cigarette smoking was about 4.3% in surveys conducted during 1999-2005.

claims.notes: This supports the presence of public health information infrastructure, but not direct prevalence of teen exposure to information.
claims.text: Publicly accessible surveillance infrastructure on youth tobacco exists through CDC's GTSSData system, which CDC describes as covering more than 20 tobacco-data topics across four survey families.

limitations: The request asks for the prevalence of public health information on smoking in Asian teens, but the retrieved evidence primarily measures tobacco-use prevalence and tobacco-surveillance coverage, not exposure to or prevalence of public health information itself.
The evidence is region-level, largely for the WHO South-East Asia Region, not all of Asia.
Some source records are based on authoritative web snippets and landing pages rather than downloaded raw microdata inside this container.
The figures combine surveys from different years and methods; direct cross-source comparison should be cautious.
The artifact is a conservative extraction ledger, not a full systematic review.
recommended_format: Use the CSV artifact as a compact evidence table; treat findings as regional tobacco-use prevalence evidence and survey-coverage metadata rather than a direct measure of 'public health info prevalence.'

sources.label: WHO global report on trends in prevalence of tobacco use 2000-2024 and projections 2025-2030
sources.notes: Web evidence included a direct PDF bitstream URL and snippet text stating the South-East Asia Region has about 11 million adolescent tobacco users aged 13-15 years, about 30% of the global total; figure text also reported average prevalence about 11% based on surveys conducted 2012-2022 and applied to 2021 populations.

sources.label: CDC MMWR 2006: Use of Cigarettes and Other Tobacco Products Among Students Aged 13-15 Years — Worldwide, 1999-2005
sources.notes: CDC/WHO GYTS summary reported South-East Asian regional prevalence of 12.9% for any tobacco use and 4.3% for cigarette smoking among students aged 13-15 years.

sources.label: CDC GTSSData portal
sources.notes: Portal description states GTSSData houses four survey families and over 20 tobacco-related data topics including tobacco use, advertising, cessation, secondhand smoke, school policies, and perceptions.

summary: Available public evidence located through WHO and CDC supports a narrow estimate about adolescent tobacco-use prevalence and the existence of public surveillance infrastructure in Asia, especially the WHO South-East Asia Region. The strongest directly reproducible figures found here indicate that WHO reported about 11 million adolescent tobacco users aged 13-15 years in the South-East Asia Region, representing about 30% of the global total, with an average current tobacco-use prevalence of about 11% based on surveys conducted during 2012-2022 and applied to 2021 populations. Older CDC/WHO GYTS reporting for 1999-2005 reported South-East Asian regional prevalence around 12.9% for any tobacco use and 4.3% for cigarette smoking among students aged 13-15 years. CDC also documents a standing public data system covering more than 20 tobacco-data topics via GTSSData.
title: Public evidence ledger for adolescent smoking-related public health information in Asia
